pageContent:
Git Commands Reference Guide

1. Repository Setup

git init - Initialize a new Git repository in the current folder.

git clone <repository_url> - Download a repository from a remote source like GitHub.

2. Checking Status

git status - Show the status of files (modified, staged, untracked).

git log - View commit history.

git log --oneline - View compact commit history.

3. Staging and Committing

git add <file> - Add a specific file to staging area.

git add . - Add all files to staging area.

git add . - Add all files to staging area.

git commit -m 'message' - Commit staged files with a message.

4. Branching

git branch - List all branches.

git branch <branch_name> - Create a new branch.

git checkout <branch_name> - Switch to a branch.

git checkout -b <branch_name> - Create and switch to a new branch.

git merge <branch_name> - Merge another branch into current branch.

5. Working with Remote Repositories

5. Working with Remote Repositories

git remote add origin <repo_url> - Connect local repository to a remote repository.

git remote -v - View remote repository URLs.

git push origin main - Push commits to the remote repository.

git push -u origin main - Push and set upstream branch.

git pull origin main - Fetch and merge changes from remote repository.

git fetch - Download objects and refs from another repository.

6. Undo Changes

6. Undo Changes

git restore <file> - Discard changes in working directory.

git reset <file> - Unstage a staged file.

git reset --soft HEAD~1 - Undo last commit but keep changes staged.

git reset --hard HEAD~1 - Remove last commit and changes permanently.

7. Removing Files

git rm <file> - Remove a file from repository.

git rm --cached <file> - Remove file from Git but keep it locally.

8. .gitignore Usage

touch .gitignore - Create a .gitignore file.

touch .gitignore - Create a .gitignore file.

echo 'node_modules/' >> .gitignore - Add node_modules to ignore list.

9. Viewing Differences

git diff - Show file differences not yet staged.

git diff --staged - Show staged changes.

10. Helpful Commands

git stash - Temporarily save changes without committing.

git stash pop - Reapply stashed changes.

git tag - List tags in repository.

git tag v1.0 - Create a tag version.
